diary:
Woke up in Bothell to that bright, early-June kind of sun that makes everything feel forgivable. I made espresso, did a quick tidy, and checked my schedule like I was trying to talk myself into a good mood before the day even started. Work at Sammamish flew by—waiting tables can be its own little comedy set, especially when I’m refilling water and resetting my brain between rushes. Around Pike Place Market, the air felt lively, and I grabbed a quick lunch and coffee without slowing down too much. After that, I wandered out to Gas Works Park for birdwatching, letting the clear sky and the calls of the birds drown out everything that usually nags at me.

By late afternoon, The Crocodile in Belltown was buzzing for the USA vs Wales match. My friends and I argued about plays and laughed like we meant it. After dinner back near Pike Place, I drove home, practiced a couple bits with espresso, and finally let the day land. I feel… lighter than I expected.
thought_process: I planned my day around momentum: get set up early for the lunch rush, then deliberately give myself a breather before the afternoon slips away. The Light Rail closure didn’t hit me directly since I drove, but it still nudged my timing—less chance of unpredictable backups downtown meant I could keep my Pike Place stop quick and efficient. I picked birdwatching at Gas Works Park because I needed a reset that wasn’t another screen or another chore; the sunny weather made it feel restorative instead of like “taking time off” for its own sake. The World Cup match shaped the social part of my day—bars opening early gave me permission to linger, and the shared commentary made my mood spike. Practicing bits at home later felt smoother, like I’d collected material and calm at the same time.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
